title: Add PR-level CI for FORGE
description:
FORGE currently has no PR-level CI workflow, so PR #4 (cycle-002 sprint-01 ir-0.2.0-bundle) could not receive pre-merge checks. Sprint 01 used local verification plus review/audit as the merge gate.

Add a PR workflow that runs at minimum:
- npm run test:unit
- npm run test:all

Triggers: pull_request / push as appropriate for the repo's PR-flow conventions.

Context: surfaced 2026-05-27 during cycle-002 sprint-01 ir-0.2.0-bundle release sequence. Local 777/792 pass + Bridgebuilder review APPROVED + Paranoid Cypherpunk Auditor PASS were the substitute gate; this is acceptable for a contract-surface change but should not be the long-term default.

title: Fix dormant post-merge workflow branch trigger
description:
.github/workflows/post-merge.yml is configured for 'branches: [main]', but the repo default branch is 'master'. As a result, the post-merge workflow did not run after Sprint 01 merge commit 16451951f48a25d0741fd27525c9d9066abf8907 (PR #4, 'cycle-002 sprint-01 ir-0.2.0-bundle').

Update the workflow trigger to match 'master' or otherwise align the repo branch strategy.

Surfaced 2026-05-27 during the cycle-002 sprint-01 release sequence. Pre-existing repo defect, not introduced by Sprint 01. Every recent run in 'gh run list' is 'Dependabot Updates'; 'Post-Merge Pipeline' has been dormant for an unknown duration prior to this discovery.

Suggested fix: change line 5 of .github/workflows/post-merge.yml from 'branches: [main]' to 'branches: [master, main]' (covers both names), or settle on one canonical default-branch name and align everything accordingly.

Verification after fix: push a no-op commit to master (or merge a trivial PR) and confirm 'gh run list --workflow="Post-Merge Pipeline"' shows a new run.

title: Reconcile FORGE construct snapshot fixtures with current emitter output
description:
Sprint 01 dry-run found fixtures/forge-snapshots-{tremor,corona,breath}.json are stale/orphan documentation fixtures. Regeneration introduces unrelated serializeProfile field renames and usefulness_score normalization drift in addition to expected IR/Lane 1 fields. Decide whether to modernize, retire, or reconnect these fixtures to tests in a dedicated fixture hygiene task.

Context:
- Sprint cycle-002-sprint-01-ir-0.2.0-bundle deferred FR-7 / T-E1.
- Operator authorization 2026-05-27: defer rather than bundle the broader drift into the IR 0.2.0 commit set.
- No current test consumes these fixtures.
- Drift categories observed: serializeProfile field renames (cadence.median_gap_ms/cv -> median_ms/jitter_coefficient; noise.spike_ratio -> spike_rate; density.stream_count -> sensor_count); usefulness_score going from 0.0594 to null (analyze() invocation signature change); plus expected IR 0.2.0 + Lane 1 additions.

Options to consider in the dedicated task:
1. Modernize: regenerate fully with current emitter output, accept the broader diff.
2. Retire: delete fixtures; remove README references; replace with generated test fixtures if any consumer surfaces.
3. Reconnect: wire fixtures back into a snapshot test so drift is detected automatically.
